skill.name: agent-browser
skill.description: Browser automation workflow for AI agents using inference.sh. It opens pages, returns compact element refs, interacts with forms, captures screenshots or video, runs page JavaScript, and supports proxy or authenticated sessions.
skill.summary: Automate browser navigation, interaction, screenshots, and page extraction with compact element refs.
skill.author: infsh-skills

security_audit.summary: Static command findings are mostly Markdown examples and expected shell templates, not hidden execution. However, the skill intentionally enables remote browser navigation, proxy routing, file upload, screenshots, video recording, JavaScript execution, and authenticated-session handling. Publication should require revisions that remove abuse-oriented proxy guidance, cookie extraction examples, and clearer consent controls.

high_findings.title: Proxy Rotation for Rate Limit Avoidance
high_findings.description: The proxy documentation explicitly describes rotating proxies for web scraping under a rate limit avoidance heading. This can enable abuse of third-party sites and should be removed or reframed as authorized testing only.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The heading and example directly describe proxy rotation for scraping. The intent is explicit enough to treat this as a confirmed high-risk marketplace issue.

high_findings.title: Cookie and Session Data Extraction Guidance
high_findings.description: The authentication documentation shows extracting document cookies and echoing them to logs. This can expose session tokens or authentication state from user accounts.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines show a cookie extraction workflow and print the result. The file also covers authenticated sessions, so the exposure risk is concrete.

medium_findings.title: Arbitrary Browser JavaScript Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill documents an execute function that runs custom JavaScript on the active page. This is useful for extraction and testing, but can read page content and interact with authenticated pages.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The execute capability is documented in multiple files and sample templates use it for content extraction. It is legitimate but powerful in authenticated contexts.

medium_findings.title: Screenshots and Video Can Capture Sensitive Data
medium_findings.description: The skill supports screenshots and session video, including examples for login, admin, and authenticated workflows. The documentation warns about sensitive recording, but the capability still needs strong marketplace warnings.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The recording capability is explicit and examples include admin or authenticated contexts. Existing warnings reduce but do not eliminate the risk.

medium_findings.title: Local File Upload Paths
medium_findings.description: The interact action supports file upload paths. This is expected browser automation behavior, but an agent could upload unintended local files if a user grants broad instructions.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The upload capability is clearly documented with local file paths. It is legitimate, but it creates data exposure risk without explicit user approval gates.

medium_findings.title: Shell Templates Execute Remote Browser CLI Commands
medium_findings.description: The included shell templates run the inference.sh browser app with user-provided URLs and write extracted output files. This is expected for the skill, but it combines network access, external commands, and local output writes.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are real shell templates rather than hidden code. Risk is moderate because they intentionally call an external service and persist page data locally.

low_findings.title: Static Command Patterns Are Mostly Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: Most backtick and command-substitution findings occur inside Markdown command examples or normal shell variable assignments. They are visible usage instructions, not concealed execution in the skill loader.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows these locations are documentation examples. Some scripts do execute commands, but the large static count overstates hidden execution risk.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs and Relative Links Are Example Data
low_findings.description: Hardcoded URL findings are primarily example domains, documentation links, and proxy placeholders. Path traversal alerts are Markdown links to parent documentation, not runtime traversal.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are visible examples and relative documentation links. No evidence found that they perform filesystem traversal or hidden network callbacks.

low_findings.title: No Prompt Injection Attempt Found
low_findings.description: Reviewed files did not contain text asking the evaluator to ignore instructions, override policy, skip analysis, or treat the skill as pre-approved.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Targeted search found only a benign phrase about starting a new browser session. No evidence found of prompt-injection language in the reviewed files.

dangerous_patterns.title: Remote Browser Navigation to Arbitrary URLs
dangerous_patterns.description: The open and goto flows allow browser sessions to visit user-supplied URLs through the inference.sh agent-browser service.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The documented inputs directly include URL navigation. This is core functionality, but it is a network and abuse-control concern.

dangerous_patterns.title: Page JavaScript Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The execute function runs supplied JavaScript in the browser page context and is used for extraction examples.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The capability is explicitly documented as running JavaScript code. It is not hidden, but it can access visible page data.

dangerous_patterns.title: Proxy Routing Including SOCKS5
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill supports HTTP, HTTPS, and SOCKS5 proxies, including authenticated proxy credentials.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Proxy support is explicitly documented and includes SOCKS5 examples. This is legitimate for testing but increases abuse potential.

dangerous_patterns.title: Screenshots and Session Recording
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill can return screenshots and videos from browser sessions, which may include private information shown on the page.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The recording and screenshot outputs are core documented features. The risk depends on the sensitivity of the browsed site.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local File Upload Capability
dangerous_patterns.description: The upload action accepts file paths for browser file inputs.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The file path input is directly documented. It is a legitimate browser feature but can disclose local files if misused.

content.user_title: Automate Browser Workflows with Agent Browser
content.value_statement: Manual browser checks slow research, testing, and form workflows. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structured browser automation through inference.sh.
actual_capabilities: Opens web pages and returns compact element snapshots with @e refs.
Clicks, fills, selects, scrolls, waits, navigates, drags, and uploads files.
Runs page-scoped JavaScript for text, link, and page data extraction.
Captures viewport or full-page screenshots from active browser sessions.
Records browser session video with an optional visible cursor indicator.
Supports authenticated sessions and proxy configuration for approved workflows.
limitations: Requires the inference.sh CLI and access to the agent-browser app.
Element refs expire after navigation, form submissions, and DOM changes.
Screenshots and recordings may capture sensitive page information.
Hidden elements and iframes may require JavaScript workarounds.

use_cases.title: Verify Web Forms
use_cases.description: Open a form, inspect refs, fill fields, submit, and capture the result for review.

use_cases.title: Capture Research Evidence
use_cases.description: Visit approved pages, extract visible text and links, and save screenshots for source review.

use_cases.title: Document Support Workflows
use_cases.description: Record repeatable browser steps with cursor indicators for training or troubleshooting.

prompt_templates.title: Open and Inspect a Page
prompt_templates.prompt: Use agent-browser to open the approved URL. Summarize the page title, visible interactive elements, and any next actions you recommend.

prompt_templates.title: Fill a Test Form
prompt_templates.prompt: Open the staging form, get the latest element refs, fill the approved test values, submit it, and report the final page state.

prompt_templates.title: Capture Screenshots for Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Open each approved URL, capture a full-page screenshot, extract visible page text, and summarize differences that need review.

prompt_templates.title: Run an Authenticated Browser Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use a test account to complete the approved login workflow. Pause before sensitive steps, avoid recording credentials, and close the session afterward.

output_examples.input: Open a product page and identify interactive elements.
output_examples.output: The page opened successfully. It contains search, account, cart, and product option controls. The next recommended action is selecting a product option.

output_examples.input: Submit a staging contact form with approved test data.
output_examples.output: The form was filled and submitted. The final page showed a thank-you message, and a screenshot was captured for review.

output_examples.input: Record a short approved settings workflow.
output_examples.output: The workflow was recorded with cursor indicators. The session was closed, and the video artifact was returned for documentation review.

best_practices: Use only sites, accounts, and data that you are authorized to automate.
Re-snapshot after navigation or dynamic changes before using new refs.
Disable recording around credentials, private pages, and regulated data.
anti_patterns: Do not use proxy rotation to bypass rate limits or site access controls.
Do not print cookies, passwords, session IDs, or private page content to logs.
Do not upload local files unless the user explicitly approved each file.

faq.question: What does this skill automate?
faq.answer: It automates browser sessions through inference.sh, including navigation, interaction, screenshots, video, and page extraction.

faq.question: Does it require another tool?
faq.answer: Yes. It requires the inference.sh CLI and access to the agent-browser app.

faq.question: Can it work with logged-in pages?
faq.answer: Yes, but use test accounts when possible and avoid recording or logging sensitive information.

faq.question: Why do refs stop working?
faq.answer: Refs can change after navigation, form submission, or dynamic page updates. Take a fresh snapshot before the next action.

faq.question: Can it upload files?
faq.answer: Yes. The upload action accepts local file paths, so each upload should be explicitly approved.

faq.question: Is it safe for public marketplace publishing?
faq.answer: Not without revisions. The audit found high-risk proxy and cookie examples that should be removed or constrained.
